title: FM Programming Changes: Saturday 1 o’clock lineup starting Oct. 5
summary: Thistle and Shamrock from NPR is ending its run and will no longer be available to NPR stations after the first weekend of October. Thistle and Shamrock has become a mainstay of our Saturday afternoon KUAC soundtrack, and it will be missed. Although the host, Fiona Ritchie is retiring, it is not the...

title: US Navy apologizes for burning and bombarding the village of Kake in 1869
summary: Rear Admiral Mark Sucato issues a formal apology for the 1869 bombardment of Kake at the village’s community center on Saturday, Sept. 21, 2024. (Screenshot from Sealaska Heritage Institute’s livestream) Over 150 years have passed since the U.S. Navy bombed Kake, a Tlingit village in Southeast Alaska. Navy representatives visited this weekend to formally apologize for the winter attack, which left many people to starve or die of exposure after the village was destroyed. A...

title: October 2024: The Kenai Peninsula’s Autumnal Angling Finale
summary: As the Kenai Peninsula dons its autumnal cloak of fiery reds, deep oranges, and golden yellows, October marks a reflective yet invigorating period for fishing enthusiasts. This month is celebrated not just for the spectacle of nature’s transition but also for the quieter, yet profoundly rewarding fishing opportunities it presents. As the bustling summer c...

title: The North Harbor parking lot closed for parking from September 29th through October 5th.
summary: The North Harbor parking lot will be undergoing maintenance and will be closed for parking from September 29th through October 5th Please note that any vehicles left in the lot during this time will be towed at the owner’s expense.
